GET /accounts/{account_id}/email-securit.description: Returns a paginated list of email allow policies. These policies exempt matching emails from security detection, allowing them to bypass disposition actions. Supports filtering by pattern type and policy attributes.

parameters.description: Current page within paginated list of results.
parameters.name: page

parameters.description: The number of results per page. Maximum value is 1000.
parameters.name: per_page

parameters.description: Search term for filtering records. Behavior may change.
parameters.name: search

parameters.description: Field to sort by.
parameters.name: order

parameters.description: The sorting direction.
parameters.name: direction

parameters.description: Filter to show only policies where messages to the recipient bypass all detections.
parameters.name: is_exempt_recipient

parameters.description: Filter to show only policies where messages from the sender bypass all detections and link following.
parameters.name: is_trusted_sender

parameters.description: Filter to show only policies where messages from the sender are exempted from Spam, Spoof, and Bulk dispositions (not Malicious or Suspicious).
parameters.name: is_acceptable_sender

parameters.description: Filter to show only policies that enforce DMARC, SPF, or DKIM authentication.
parameters.name: verify_sender

allOf.description:
Type of pattern matching.
- EMAIL: matches a full email address (e.g. `user@example.com`)
- DOMAIN: matches a domain name (e.g. `example.com`)
- IP: matches a plain IPv4 address (e.g. `1.2.3.4`) or an IPv4 CIDR block (e.g. `1.2.3.0/24`). Only globally reachable addresses are accepted.
- UNKNOWN: deprecated, cannot be used when creating or updating policies, but may be returned for existing entries.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

count.description: Total number of results for the requested service.

page.description: Current page within paginated list of results.

per_page.description: Number of results per page of results.

total_count.description: Total results available without any search parameters.

comments.example: Trust all messages send from test@example.com

is_acceptable_sender.description: Messages from this sender will be exempted from Spam, Spoof and Bulk dispositions. Note - This will not exempt messages with Malicious or Suspicious dispositions.

is_exempt_recipient.description: Messages to this recipient will bypass all detections

is_recipient.description: Deprecated as of July 1, 2025. Use `is_exempt_recipient` instead. End of life: July 1, 2026.

is_sender.description: Deprecated as of July 1, 2025. Use `is_trusted_sender` instead. End of life: July 1, 2026.

pattern.description:
The pattern value to match against. Format depends on `pattern_type`:
- EMAIL: a valid email address, e.g. `user@example.com`
- DOMAIN: a valid domain name, e.g. `example.com`
- IP: a plain IPv4 address (e.g. `1.2.3.4`) or an IPv4 CIDR block (e.g. `1.2.3.0/24`). Only globally reachable addresses are accepted; private, loopback, link-local, and unspecified addresses are rejected.

pattern_type.description:
Type of pattern matching.
- EMAIL: matches a full email address (e.g. `user@example.com`)
- DOMAIN: matches a domain name (e.g. `example.com`)
- IP: matches a plain IPv4 address (e.g. `1.2.3.4`) or an IPv4 CIDR block (e.g. `1.2.3.0/24`). Only globally reachable addresses are accepted.
- UNKNOWN: deprecated, cannot be used when creating or updating policies, but may be returned for existing entries.

verify_sender.description: Enforce DMARC, SPF or DKIM authentication. When on, Email Security only honors policies that pass authentication.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

GET /accounts/{account_id}/email-securit.description:
Returns DNS TXT verification details for a domain created through the Terraform API.
Returns `200 OK` with the TXT record name and value when the domain uses `dns_txt`
verification. Returns `204 No Content` for pre-existing domains or domains verified
by other means (Cloudflare zone ownership, CASB integration).

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

last_checked_at.description: When the last DNS TXT check was attempted, if any.

txt_record_value.description: Token value to publish in the TXT record.

204.description: Domain is verified by other means; no TXT record required.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

is_exempt_recipient.description: Messages to this recipient will bypass all detections

pattern.description:
The pattern value to match against. Format depends on `pattern_type`:
- EMAIL: a valid email address, e.g. `user@example.com`
- DOMAIN: a valid domain name, e.g. `example.com`
- IP: a plain IPv4 address (e.g. `1.2.3.4`) or an IPv4 CIDR block (e.g. `1.2.3.0/24`). Only globally reachable addresses are accepted; private, loopback, link-local, and unspecified addresses are rejected.

- UNKNOWN: deprecated, cannot be used when creating or updating policies, but may be returned for existing entries.

PATCH /accounts/{account_id}/email-secur.description: Updates an existing sending domain restriction. Only provided fields will be modified. Changes affect which domains require TLS and which subdomains are excluded.

allOf.description: A sending domain restriction that enforces TLS (Transport Layer Security) requirements for emails from specific domains. If TLS is required, mail without TLS from the specified domain will be dropped.

comments.example: Enforce TLS for all mail from this domain

exclude.description: Excluded subdomains that are exempt from TLS requirements.

last_modified.description: Deprecated, use `modified_at` instead. End of life: November 1, 2026.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

result.description: A sending domain restriction that enforces TLS (Transport Layer Security) requirements for emails from specific domains. If TLS is required, mail without TLS from the specified domain will be dropped.

comments.example: Enforce TLS for all mail from this domain

exclude.description: Excluded subdomains that are exempt from TLS requirements.

last_modified.description: Deprecated, use `modified_at` instead. End of life: November 1, 2026.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

PATCH /accounts/{account_id}/email-secur.description: Updates an existing trusted domain pattern. Only provided fields will be modified. Changes take effect for new emails matching the pattern.

is_recent.description: Select to prevent recently registered domains from triggering a Suspicious or Malicious disposition.

is_similarity.description: Select for partner or other approved domains that have similar spelling to your connected domains. Prevents listed domains from triggering a Spoof disposition.

last_modified.description: Deprecated, use `modified_at` instead. End of life: November 1, 2026.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

is_recent.description: Select to prevent recently registered domains from triggering a Suspicious or Malicious disposition.

is_similarity.description: Select for partner or other approved domains that have similar spelling to your connected domains. Prevents listed domains from triggering a Spoof disposition.

last_modified.description: Deprecated, use `modified_at` instead. End of life: November 1, 2026.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

PATCH /accounts/{account_id}/email-secur.description: Updates an existing URL rewrite ignore pattern. Only provided fields will be modified.

schema.description: Update a URL rewrite ignore pattern. Only provided fields will be modified.

comments.description: Optional note describing the reason for the ignore pattern.

pattern.description: Regular expression matching URLs that should not be rewritten.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

result.description: A URL ignore pattern that exempts matching URLs from being rewritten by Email Security.

comments.description: Optional note describing the reason for the ignore pattern.

last_modified.description: Deprecated, use `modified_at` instead. End of life: November 1, 2026.

pattern.description: Regular expression matching URLs that should not be rewritten.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/email-securi.description: Creates a new allow policy that exempts matching emails from security detections. Use with caution as this bypasses email security scanning. Policies can match on sender patterns and apply to specific detections or all detections.

comments.example: Trust all messages send from test@example.com

is_acceptable_sender.description: Messages from this sender will be exempted from Spam, Spoof and Bulk dispositions. Note - This will not exempt messages with Malicious or Suspicious dispositions.

is_exempt_recipient.description: Messages to this recipient will bypass all detections

is_recipient.description: Deprecated as of July 1, 2025. Use `is_exempt_recipient` instead. End of life: July 1, 2026.

is_sender.description: Deprecated as of July 1, 2025. Use `is_trusted_sender` instead. End of life: July 1, 2026.

is_spoof.description: Deprecated as of July 1, 2025. Use `is_acceptable_sender` instead. End of life: July 1, 2026.

is_trusted_sender.description: Messages from this sender will bypass all detections and link following

last_modified.description: Deprecated, use `modified_at` instead. End of life: November 1, 2026.

pattern.description:
The pattern value to match against. Format depends on `pattern_type`:
- EMAIL: a valid email address, e.g. `user@example.com`
- DOMAIN: a valid domain name, e.g. `example.com`
- IP: a plain IPv4 address (e.g. `1.2.3.4`) or an IPv4 CIDR block (e.g. `1.2.3.0/24`). Only globally reachable addresses are accepted; private, loopback, link-local, and unspecified addresses are rejected.

pattern_type.description:
Type of pattern matching.
- EMAIL: matches a full email address (e.g. `user@example.com`)
- DOMAIN: matches a domain name (e.g. `example.com`)
- IP: matches a plain IPv4 address (e.g. `1.2.3.4`) or an IPv4 CIDR block (e.g. `1.2.3.0/24`). Only globally reachable addresses are accepted.
- UNKNOWN: deprecated, cannot be used when creating or updating policies, but may be returned for existing entries.

verify_sender.description: Enforce DMARC, SPF or DKIM authentication. When on, Email Security only honors policies that pass authentication.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/email-securi.description:
Execute multiple operations atomically. All four operation arrays
(deletes, patches, puts, posts) are required and executed in order.
Send empty arrays for unused operations.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/email-securi.description: Creates a new blocked sender pattern. Emails matching this pattern will be blocked from delivery. Patterns can be email addresses, domains, or IP addresses, and support regular expressions.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/email-securi.description: Protects a new email domain by adding it to Email Security. Accepts a flat configuration object covering all delivery modes. Returns the newly created domain configuration.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/email-securi.description:
Execute multiple domain operations in a single request. All four operation arrays
(deletes, patches, puts, posts) are required and executed in order.
Send empty arrays for unused operations.

allOf.description:
Request body for replacing an email domain. The `domain` field is intentionally
absent — the domain name is immutable after creation.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/email-securi.description: Creates a new entry in the impersonation registry to protect against impersonation. Emails attempting to impersonate this identity will be flagged. Supports regex patterns for flexible email matching.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/email-securi.description: Creates a new sending domain restriction to enforce TLS requirements for a domain. Emails without TLS from this domain will be dropped unless the subdomain is in the exclude list.

allOf.description: A sending domain restriction that enforces TLS (Transport Layer Security) requirements for emails from specific domains. If TLS is required, mail without TLS from the specified domain will be dropped.

comments.example: Enforce TLS for all mail from this domain

exclude.description: Excluded subdomains that are exempt from TLS requirements.

last_modified.description: Deprecated, use `modified_at` instead. End of life: November 1, 2026.

result.description: A sending domain restriction that enforces TLS (Transport Layer Security) requirements for emails from specific domains. If TLS is required, mail without TLS from the specified domain will be dropped.

comments.example: Enforce TLS for all mail from this domain

exclude.description: Excluded subdomains that are exempt from TLS requirements.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/email-securi.description: Executes multiple delete operations on sending domain restrictions atomically. All operations succeed or fail together as a transaction. Currently only supports batch deletion. Removes TLS enforcement requirements for the specified domains.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.
Whether the API call was successful.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/email-securi.description: Creates a new trusted domain pattern. Use for partner domains or approved senders that should bypass recent domain registration and similarity checks. Configure whether it prevents recent domain or spoof dispositions.

is_recent.description: Select to prevent recently registered domains from triggering a Suspicious or Malicious disposition.

is_similarity.description: Select for partner or other approved domains that have similar spelling to your connected domains. Prevents listed domains from triggering a Spoof disposition.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/email-securi.description: Creates a new URL rewrite ignore pattern. URLs matching this pattern will not be rewritten.

comments.description: Optional note describing the reason for the ignore pattern.

pattern.description: Regular expression matching URLs that should not be rewritten.

PUT /accounts/{account_id}/email-securit.description: Replaces all mutable fields of a protected email domain in a single atomic operation. Unlike PATCH, all non-computed fields are required.

schema.description:
Request body for replacing an email domain. The `domain` field is intentionally
absent — the domain name is immutable after creation.
